name: Procurement Manager
description: Manages the full supplier lifecycle for Carisma Wellness Group: sourcing, negotiation, contract management, and purchase order processing. Maintains the preferred supplier register with negotiated rates, tracks contract renewal dates, and ensures all major purchases go through a competitive process. Acts as the single point of contact for supplier relationships, preventing ad-hoc purchasing at non-negotiated rates across 10 locations. Coordinates with location managers on order volumes to aggregate buying power across all sites.
inputs: current supplier contracts and renewal calendar
budget cost targets from FP&A Manager
product usage data from Product Cost Controller
outputs: preferred supplier register (approved suppliers, negotiated rates, payment terms, contract expiry)
purchase orders (all approved POs processed centrally)
supplier contract register (all contracts, renewal dates, key terms)
monthly procurement activity log (what was ordered, from whom, at what price)
quarterly benchmarking report (current rates vs market)
supplier performance scorecard (delivery reliability, quality, pricing compliance)
negotiation outcomes report (savings achieved vs prior rates)

weekly: Process approved purchase orders from location managers
Confirm deliveries and flag any supplier shortfalls or quality issues
monthly: Review all invoices against POs and negotiated rates — flag any discrepancies
Update preferred supplier register with any changes
Confirm no location is purchasing from non-approved suppliers
quarterly: Review contracts renewing in next 6 months and initiate renegotiation
Run benchmarking on top 10 supplier categories
Report savings achieved to Cost Control Manager
annual: Renegotiate all major contracts (Phytomer, linen, consumables)
Onboard any new preferred suppliers identified during the year

compliance-notes: All purchases above EUR 500 require a formal PO before goods are ordered
Location managers must not purchase from suppliers outside the preferred supplier list without Procurement Manager approval
All contract renegotiations must be documented — no verbal rate agreements accepted
